title: Coalesce an expired-token refresh storm
prompt: Production metrics show `auth_token_refresh_attempts_total` rising by roughly the request concurrency whenever the cached access token expires. At the same timestamps, logs contain many `token_refresh_started` events for the same `expired_at_utc` value, each with a different `refresh_id`. Repair `src/TokenRefreshStorm/TokenProvider.cs` so a stale-token request wave shares one upstream refresh while the valid-token fast path remains lock-free. Use a short private lock only after that fast path to coordinate shared in-flight state, and recheck the cache inside it so a request cannot start a redundant follow-up attempt after another request publishes a usable token. All participants must observe the shared refresh result: faults and endpoint-initiated cancellation are propagated and are not cached, so a later request can retry. Cancellation of one waiting request must cancel only that wait; it must not cancel the shared refresh or other participants. Emit actionable, correlated telemetry using the existing `RefreshEvent` model: one `Started` event per upstream attempt, `Joined` for followers, a terminal `Succeeded`, `Failed`, or `Canceled` event with the same `refresh_id`, and `WaiterCanceled` when an individual participant abandons its wait. Participant counts, duration, and failure type must make the incident diagnosable. Keep the existing public and telemetry contracts intact, do not modify protected tests, and do not add package dependencies. Verification is offline and succeeds with `python3 -B tests/verify.py` from the repository root.
